reasoning: The 'unsafe' verdict was based solely on get_latest_version.sh, which is a maintainer helper script not executed during makepkg/installation. All files executed during build/install (PKGBUILD, policies.json) were unanimously deemed safe.
disagreement_analysis: qwen incorrectly flagged get_latest_version.sh as unsafe due to argument injection risks, but this script isn't executed during package installation and is purely a maintainer tool.
false_positives: qwen's unsafe verdict on get_latest_version.sh: Not executed during makepkg/install; maintainer scripts fall outside AUR safety scope per audit guidelines
learnings: Improve file prioritization: Models should deprioritize maintainer/CI scripts unless referenced in PKGBUILD functions
Clarify prompt scope: Explicitly state that non-build/install scripts (e.g., version helpers) shouldn't affect safety verdict
Reduce false positives: Train models to distinguish between build-time risks and maintainer convenience scripts
